ogTitle: Moulyinning vs Mount Adams: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Moulyinning and Mount Adams.
heading: Moulyinning vs Mount Adams — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Moulyinning, Western Australia and Mount Adams, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Adams has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Moulyinning (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Moulyinning or Mount Adams safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Moulyinning has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Mount Adams scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Moulyinning scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Adams scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Moulyinning or Mount Adams?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Adams has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
